Records.description: Prescriptive-unitarity kissing-box integral for two-loop six-gluon planar massless QCD amplitudes, constructed as two one-loop boxes joined at a single cubic vertex, with a numerator of the form $n_{kb}=[[m,b,c,j]][[k,f,g,l]]$ (Eq. (eq:Ikb)). It provides the two-loop leading singularity prefactor $R_{kb}$ for the IR-finite part of the hard function. Called $I_{n,jklm}^{kb}$ in the paper (with $n=6$ the number of legs). Eight massless propagators (two boxes, one shared vertex), six massless external gluons, all distinct helicity sectors --++++, -+-+++, -++-++ computed through weight four via IBP reduction to the Henn:2025xrc planar two-loop six-point UT basis with FIRE7, KIRA3 and FiniteFlow. Contributes only to pure-YM ($N_f^0$) hard functions.
Records.location: Section 5, Eq. (eq:Ikb); Fig. fig:six-points; IBP reduction in Appendix sec: IBP reduction

Records.description: Massless planar two-loop split-double-box scalar integral: two boxes joined at a single shared vertex (no shared propagator), with eight propagators total, four per loop. Defined diagrammatically in Fig. 1b/Fig. 9 and via the cut conditions in Eqs. (4.13)-(4.14): the q-loop quadruple cut q^2 = (q-k_6)^2 = (q-k_5-k_6)^2 = (q+k_7)^2 = 0 and the p-loop quadruple cut p^2 = (p-k_2)^2 = (p-k_1-k_2)^2 = (p+k_3)^2 = 0. Each box carries three external clusters K_i (sums of consecutive gluon momenta), so the family applies for amplitudes with at least six external gluons (the canonical configuration drawn). The integral itself is not evaluated; the paper extracts its rational coefficient via an honest octa-cut that localizes both loop integrals, giving the coefficient as a sum over 4 cut solutions of products of seven tree amplitudes (Eq. 4.15).
